name: Arozian, Johannes Aaron
motto: Ambition, thou goad of all mankind.
description: First saw the light of day "on the other side" in a little country of southern Europe. When the Turks saw John in '96, they said, "Let it live," whereupon he took the first boat for America and landed here in the "Chem." course feet first. "Ohannes" is analyzing some blue "mud" from the old country and calls it copper ore. After graduation, John will return to develop the unusual mineral resources of his native land.
text:
Ambition, thou goad of all mankind.
First saw the light of day "on the other side" in a little country of southern Europe. When the Turks saw John in '96, they said, "Let it live," whereupon he took the first boat for America and landed here in the "Chem." course feet first. "Ohannes" is analyzing some blue "mud" from the old country and calls it copper ore. After graduation, John will return to develop the unusual mineral resources of his native land.

name: Bachelder, John Hutchins
motto: You may relish him more in the soldier than in the scholar.
description: Strolled leisurely into Durham in the fall of 1907, found a room in Pettee Block, then immediately went to Dover to buy a Morris chair. We believe he already had the pipe, - at any rate, the chair and pipe have ever since continued to be the chief requisites of his existence. "Bach" is taking the "Aggie" course and after completing his college work he will probably become farm foreman of the state prison farm at West Concord.
text:
You may relish him more in the soldier than in the scholar.
Strolled leisurely into Durham in the fall of 1907, found a room in Pettee Block, then immediately went to Dover to buy a Morris chair. We believe he already had the pipe, - at any rate, the chair and pipe have ever since continued to be the chief requisites of his existence. "Bach" is taking the "Aggie" course and after completing his college work he will probably become farm foreman of the state prison farm at West Concord.

name: Bennett, Leland Wilson
motto: True love is better than glory.
description: Was born in due time in the metropolis of Laconia, one of the suburbs of Concord. In the fall of 1907 he came to Durham, a serious, conscientious lad, and has ever since remained true to someone who claimed his attentions before coming to college. It is rumored that he will form a partnership after graduation. If so, good luck, "Herr," and, to use an old expression, may all your troubles be little ones.
text:
True love is better than glory.
Was born in due time in the metropolis of Laconia, one of the suburbs of Concord. In the fall of 1907 he came to Durham, a serious, conscientious lad, and has ever since remained true to someone who claimed his attentions before coming to college. It is rumored that he will form a partnership after graduation. If so, good luck, "Herr," and, to use an old expression, may all your troubles be little ones.

name: Brown, Albert Huckins
motto: There are but few who know the treasures hid in thee.
description: "Brownie" hails from Strafford and is a walking history of the town and its inhabitants. His characteristics are extreme taciturnity, moderation in eating and studious habits. "Brownie" takes the real original "Aggie" course and as a side line has been experimenting with a new variety of western grown tobacco. Here's to you, "Brownie," may you always be jolly.
text:
There are but few who know the treasures hid in thee.
"Brownie" hails from Strafford and is a walking history of the town and its inhabitants. His characteristics are extreme taciturnity, moderation in eating and studious habits. "Brownie" takes the real original "Aggie" course and as a side line has been experimenting with a new variety of western grown tobacco. Here's to you, "Brownie," may you always be jolly.

name: Brown, Charles Owen
motto: Speaks an infinite deal of nothing.
description: We won't say exactly where "C. O." came from, for we are afraid it might reflect on the town. "Brownie" is quite a talker; he talks about automobiles, he talks about money, he talks about nothing, in fact, "Cob" talks fluently on any subject, even about himself. But this same quality has made him a good Business Manager for the GRANITE, because every business man gives an "ad" in self-defense. "C. O." is a ladies' man and finds a railroad pass (to Boston) very useful in his business.
text:
Speaks an infinite deal of nothing.
We won't say exactly where "C. O." came from, for we are afraid it might reflect on the town. "Brownie" is quite a talker; he talks about automobiles, he talks about money, he talks about nothing, in fact, "Cob" talks fluently on any subject, even about himself. But this same quality has made him a good Business Manager for the GRANITE, because every business man gives an "ad" in self-defense. "C. O." is a ladies' man and finds a railroad pass (to Boston) very useful in his business.

name: Burbeck, Perry James
motto: With learned mien, He burns the midnight kerosene.
description: "Perry," like many another illustrious genius, was born in a quiet little village. He is one of those who still stick to the idea that college is a place for study, and has consequently amassed quite a knowledge of matters electrical. "Perry" is a woman hater to those who do not know him, but those who are on the inside predict him a Benedict soon after graduation. We suppose he will follow Horace Greeley's advice and migrate westward.
text:
With learned mien, He burns the midnight kerosene.
"Perry," like many another illustrious genius, was born in a quiet little village. He is one of those who still stick to the idea that college is a place for study, and has consequently amassed quite a knowledge of matters electrical. "Perry" is a woman hater to those who do not know him, but those who are on the inside predict him a Benedict soon after graduation. We suppose he will follow Horace Greeley's advice and migrate westward.

name: Carpenter, Roy Elbert
motto: He is the very pineapple of politeness.
description: Did anything good ever come from Medford? Look at him. He is a ladies' man, and can tell the name of every girl who frequents "Powder House Square," Somerville. He is an active member and attends every meeting and a great many more besides that are not regular. He is also a familiar figure at "Scotty's" afternoon "Pol. Econ." receptions. He is not much of a singer, but he can render "Hurrah for the Brown and Blue" in a manner that will cause the tears to flow down the back of your neck. He expects to become mayor of Dover.
text:
He is the very pineapple of politeness.
Did anything good ever come from Medford? Look at him. He is a ladies' man, and can tell the name of every girl who frequents "Powder House Square," Somerville. He is an active member and attends every meeting and a great many more besides that are not regular. He is also a familiar figure at "Scotty's" afternoon "Pol. Econ." receptions. He is not much of a singer, but he can render "Hurrah for the Brown and Blue" in a manner that will cause the tears to flow down the back of your neck. He expects to become mayor of Dover.

name: Colby, Arthur Samuel
motto: Bachelors have a right to be fussy.
description: He is a pretty good old soul. Most every Sunday he goes to Y. M. C. A. meeting and gives everyone a word of welcome or else selects a hymn which nobody knows. He used to take a great interest in collecting "bugs," but when he had collected one hundred and one and "Cicero" told him that eighty-three were no good, he began to lose interest in the work— but he told "Sandy" that he had derived a great deal of benefit from the course. He takes "Aggie" chiefly on account of the stenographers in that department. He will get his degree in 1911 and some day will be Secretary of Agriculture.
text:
Bachelors have a right to be fussy.
He is a pretty good old soul. Most every Sunday he goes to Y. M. C. A. meeting and gives everyone a word of welcome or else selects a hymn which nobody knows. He used to take a great interest in collecting "bugs," but when he had collected one hundred and one and "Cicero" told him that eighty-three were no good, he began to lose interest in the work— but he told "Sandy" that he had derived a great deal of benefit from the course. He takes "Aggie" chiefly on account of the stenographers in that department. He will get his degree in 1911 and some day will be Secretary of Agriculture.

name: Demerritt, Margaret
motto: Whose school-hours are all days and nights of her existence.
description: She is one of Durham's fairest maidens and is known throughout college for the fine "feeds" she gives at "Broad Acres." She attends every recitation, rain or shine, and, as a reward, draws the best of marks. She developed a keen interest in the military department two years ago. She is one of those who will surely stand by us until the last day.
text:
Whose school-hours are all days and nights of her existence.
She is one of Durham's fairest maidens and is known throughout college for the fine "feeds" she gives at "Broad Acres." She attends every recitation, rain or shine, and, as a reward, draws the best of marks. She developed a keen interest in the military department two years ago. She is one of those who will surely stand by us until the last day.

name: Drew, Mariette Alice
motto: It would talk, Lord! how it talked!
description: Who hasn't heard of "Mariette?" If there be one who hasn't, let him come forward and be introduced — it is part of his college education. "Mariette" was one of the charter members of the "With-Drew Club" — a society organized in 1907 for the "Prevention of Cruelty to Co-eds." During her Freshman year "Mariette" seriously contemplated taking the "Chem." course and, in fact, has been closely affiliated with it ever since. If "Billy" Groves' courses hold out, she expects to be in for the finish in June, 1911.

During her Freshman year "Mariette" seriously contemplated taking the "Chem." course and, in fact, has been closely affiliated with it ever since. If "Billy" Groves' courses hold out, she expects to be in for the finish in June, 1911.

name: Easterbrook, Ralph Lewis
motto: A meek, mysterious man.
description: "Ralph" came into town quietly one day and has lived quietly ever since. But "Easter" proved to be a valuable find when basketball season came. Before the game in our Sophomore year, he was the subject of much discussion and direful plotting. The Freshmen said to each other, "We must cause the sudden downfall of this foul-shooting prodigy," but "Easter" survived and won the game for 1911. The Agricultural course is "Ralph's" choice and we wish him good luck.
text:
A meek, mysterious man.
"Ralph" came into town quietly one day and has lived quietly ever since. But "Easter" proved to be a valuable find when basketball season came. Before the game in our Sophomore year, he was the subject of much discussion and direful plotting. The Freshmen said to each other, "We must cause the sudden downfall of this foul-shooting prodigy," but "Easter" survived and won the game for 1911. The Agricultural course is "Ralph's" choice and we wish him good luck.

name: Hatch, Olive Estelle
motto: A still, small voice.
description: She is not generally interested in social affairs in Durham, but once interested she feels like "jumping over the table." She has failed courses in gossip 6, 7, and 8, but otherwise her marks are a credit to the class. "Olive's" hobby is dissecting cats and writing life histories of animals. Good luck to you, Olive, keep on with your good work.
text:
A still, small voice.
She is not generally interested in social affairs in Durham, but once interested she feels like "jumping over the table." She has failed courses in gossip 6, 7, and 8, but otherwise her marks are a credit to the class. "Olive's" hobby is dissecting cats and writing life histories of animals. Good luck to you, Olive, keep on with your good work.

name: Hoben, Francis Michael
motto: If at first you don't succeed, try again.
description: He is one of the veterans of the class. He has a military record that would be hard to beat, having been on the active list four years in high school and two years here - one year in the ranks and one in the Alto section. Last year he was promoted for bravery in action and now holds the honorable position of Drum Major. Barring a slight resemblance to "Theron" "Mike" is a good director. When "Sousa" lays down the baton there will have passed away one of the most famous military men of the age.
text:
If at first you don't succeed, try again.
He is one of the veterans of the class. He has a military record that would be hard to beat, having been on the active list four years in high school and two years here - one year in the ranks and one in the Alto section. When "Sousa" lays down the baton there will have passed away one of the most famous military men of the age.

name: Judkins, Henry Forrest
motto: The farmers are the founders of civilization.
description: He was a quiet lad when he entered college in the fall of '07, but has developed wonderfully. He can now yell with exceptional vigor and make everyone else join in the good work. "Jud" certainly is the right kind of a cheer leader. This Kingston fellow is taking a sort of correspondence course here, judging from the length of time he is away doing "Aggie" stunts throughout the state. Nevertheless, when "Jud" receives his sheepskin, we predict a new authority in the agricultural profession.
text:
The farmers are the founders of civilization.
He was a quiet lad when he entered college in the fall of '07, but has developed wonderfully. He can now yell with exceptional vigor and make everyone else join in the good work. "Jud" certainly is the right kind of a cheer leader. This Kingston fellow is taking a sort of correspondence course here, judging from the length of time he is away doing "Aggie" stunts throughout the state. Nevertheless, when "Jud" receives his sheepskin, we predict a new authority in the agricultural profession.

name: Nason, Carl Eastman
motto: I am, sir, a Brother of the Angle.
description: Entered college in his dignified, matter-of-fact way and has continued thus up to the present time. The most undignified thing he ever did was to get into the Cane Rush at the beginning of our Freshman year. In this, however, his long arms counted, for he got one hand on the coveted "stick," thereby adding one point to 1911's winning total. "Deac" enjoyed his Freshman studies very much, in fact, so well that he even hesitated to register Sophomore, but finally decided to advance with the "bunch."
text:
I am, sir, a Brother of the Angle.
Entered college in his dignified, matter-of-fact way and has continued thus up to the present time. The most undignified thing he ever did was to get into the Cane Rush at the beginning of our Freshman year. In this, however, his long arms counted, for he got one hand on the coveted "stick," thereby adding one point to 1911's winning total. "Deac" enjoyed his Freshman studies very much, in fact, so well that he even hesitated to register Sophomore, but finally decided to advance with the "bunch."

name: Parker, Edward Gookin
motto: I always get the better when I argue alone.
description: "Are we in it? Well, I guess, Portsmouth, Rah! Rah!" "Gook" is the most loyal man to Portsmouth that ever stood on the wharf. Strange he should come way up here to get his military training with a navy yard just across the brink in Kittery. "Ed" is also one of the "Chem. Bunch" where he makes about an even break of everything. He expects to make a living in after life by doing secretary work and odd jobs for Dr. Randall.
text:
I always get the better when I argue alone.
"Are we in it? Well, I guess, Portsmouth, Rah! Rah!" "Gook" is the most loyal man to Portsmouth that ever stood on the wharf. Strange he should come way up here to get his military training with a navy yard just across the brink in Kittery. "Ed" is also one of the "Chem. Bunch" where he makes about an even break of everything. He expects to make a living in after life by doing secretary work and odd jobs for Dr. Randall.

name: Parker, William Folger
motto: Sensible and yet he seemeth not.
description: Look at this sweet round-faced lad. He comes from Goffstown. With possibly one or two exceptions he is the noisiest man in college. If at any time you hear a noise that sounds like a cross between a flock of geese and the College Band you may rest assured that "Bill" is somewhere in the vicinity. He believes in variety, so he has made a try at nearly every course in college. "Bill" thinks he will quiet down some day. He says he is sorry that he comes from Goffstown, but that he is going to lead a better life.
text:
Sensible and yet he seemeth not.
Look at this sweet round-faced lad. He comes from Goffstown. With possibly one or two exceptions he is the noisiest man in college. If at any time you hear a noise that sounds like a cross between a flock of geese and the College Band you may rest assured that "Bill" is somewhere in the vicinity. He believes in variety, so he has made a try at nearly every course in college. "Bill" thinks he will quiet down some day. He says he is sorry that he comes from Goffstown, but that he is going to lead a better life.

name: Bret Please
motto: Absence makes the heart grow fonder.
description: In the fall of 1907 decided that climbing telephone poles was not a good occupation, and then was here, too, so what could do but come. and the tandem were familiar sights of the roadside during our Sophomore year. When the "Hen House" was built applied for the janitor's job, but got stung; nevertheless, he succeeded in hanging around the joint for a whole year. expects to get married in June, 1911, when he and his spouse will begin a two-year engagement at the Old Howard doing fancy stunts at tandem riding.
text:
Absence makes the heart grow fonder.
In the fall of 1907 decided that climbing telephone poles was not a good occupation, and then was here, too, so what could do but come. and the tandem were familiar sights of the roadside during our Sophomore year. When the "Hen House" was built applied for the janitor's job, but got stung; nevertheless, he succeeded in hanging around the joint for a whole year. expects to get married in June, 1911, when he and his spouse will begin a two-year engagement at the Old Howard doing fancy stunts at tandem riding.

name: Leonard Emerson Pierce
motto: Why, then, do you walk as if you had swallowed a ramrod?
description: came to us from the Heart of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. Seeking a cinch course, someone suggested "E. E." and swallowed the bait. has made many friends while here, but the majority in the surrounding towns. Between trips to Dover, and other places, he has done enough work to soothe the faculty in order to be with us in June, 1911. is an awful "fusser," and will leave fond memories in the hearts of Dover's fair maidens.
text:
Why, then, do you walk as if you had swallowed a ramrod?
came to us from the Heart of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. Seeking a cinch course, someone suggested "E. E." and swallowed the bait. has made many friends while here, but the majority in the surrounding towns. Between trips to Dover, and other places, he has done enough work to soothe the faculty in order to be with us in June, 1911. is an awful "fusser," and will leave fond memories in the hearts of Dover's fair maidens.

name: Benjamin Franklin Proud
motto: Hail to the chief!
description: has been our president for three years and has worked hard to uphold the honor of 1911. He has represented us on the Varsity football team every season, and is the only man in the class who has won his numerals six times. It didn't take long to discover that engineering had no attraction for him, so he is now a thorough Arts and Science man who swears by "Billy" and all his courses.
text:
Hail to the chief!
has been our president for three years and has worked hard to uphold the honor of 1911. He has represented us on the Varsity football team every season, and is the only man in the class who has won his numerals six times. It didn't take long to discover that engineering had no attraction for him, so he is now a thorough Arts and Science man who swears by "Billy" and all his courses.

name: Quimby, Waldo Hutchinson
motto: Perseverance wins success.
description: Although was so unfortunate as to be obliged to stay out of college a year, he has returned and again joined the ranks of 1911, which simply serves to show his good judgment. is one of those who never go to Newmarket, but instead writes a "certain" letter every day. It is rumored that will form a partnership after he gets his degree.
text:
Perseverance wins success.
Although was so unfortunate as to be obliged to stay out of college a year, he has returned and again joined the ranks of 1911, which simply serves to show his good judgment. is one of those who never go to Newmarket, but instead writes a "certain" letter every day. It is rumored that will form a partnership after he gets his degree.

name: Scott, Bessie Amanda
motto: Maidens should be mild and meek, Swift to hear and slow to speak.
description: Blew in from the Green Mountain state in 1906, but there is nothing green about her except her last year's suit. Her distinguishing characteristics are a habit of electing German courses and a particular fondness for "Pie." She is a patron of athletics and has herself made several noteworthy "cross-country" records.
text:

Her distinguishing characteristics are a habit of electing German courses and a particular fondness for "Pie." She is a patron of athletics and has herself made several noteworthy "cross-country" records.

name: Stark, Eldon Eugene
motto: As cold as cucumbers.
description: Blew into town and settled in the "Block." He has been blowing most of the time since, that is, when he hasn't had such a "grouch" that he couldn't or wouldn't. He is a society man and in demand at Smith Hall and Newmarket. He is a "fusser" and does not bite.
text:
As cold as cucumbers.
Blew into town and settled in the "Block." He has been blowing most of the time since, that is, when he hasn't had such a "grouch" that he couldn't or wouldn't. He is a society man and in demand at Smith Hall and Newmarket. He is a "fusser" and does not bite.

name: Tuttle, Harry Benjamin
motto: Up from the meadows green with corn.
description: Hails from Atkinson (wherever that is) and is taking the "Aggie" course. Whatever his life work may be, "Tut" will try to find time to put into practice some of "Art's" course in Physics, in which he took such an interest during his Sophomore year. Tuttle has been a consistent worker while here and we wish him the best of success.
text:

Hails from Atkinson (wherever that is) and is taking the "Aggie" course. Whatever his life work may be, "Tut" will try to find time to put into practice some of "Art's" course in Physics, in which he took such an interest during his Sophomore year. Tuttle has been a consistent worker while here and we wish him the best of success.

name: Warner, William Pearl Jr.
motto: Full many a flower is born to blush unseen.
description: This boy is a promising Granger and has also been mayor of the town from which he comes, for many years. If it is ever hot enough for him to get his degree, "Bill" expects to retire to his native home and make the taking of summer boarders his chief occupation.
text:
Full many a flower is born to blush unseen.
This boy is a promising Granger and has also been mayor of the town from which he comes, for many years. If it is ever hot enough for him to get his degree, "Bill" expects to retire to his native home and make the taking of summer boarders his chief occupation.

name: Whittemore, Charles Farnum
motto: Silence is golden.
description: Although it is impossible to get "Doc" up until 7.53 o'clock in the morning, a glance at his college record suffices to show that he hasn't been asleep all of the time. "Whit" has chosen chemistry for his life's work.
text:
Silence is golden.
Although it is impossible to get "Doc" up until 7.53 o'clock in the morning, a glance at his college record suffices to show that he hasn't been asleep all of the time. "Whit" has chosen chemistry for his life's work.
